notes: Run 2026-07-01. 15 clues across: Ancient History (2), Dates/Documents (1), European History (3), Historical Figures (2), Medieval & Renaissance (1), Revolutions (1), U.S. History (4), World Wars (1). Tier: $200×1, $400×4, $600×5, $800×3, $1000×1. Focus on thin subcategories (Ancient History, Dates/Documents, World Wars). $1000 = Kalmar Union (from Copenhagen doc).

questions.clue: Following a 13-day siege during the Texas Revolution, Mexican forces under President-General Santa Anna retook this former Franciscan mission near San Antonio in March 1836.

citations.title: Battle of the Alamo
citations.snippet: The Battle of the Alamo was a pivotal event and military engagement in the Texas Revolution. Following a 13-day siege, Mexican troops under President General Antonio López de Santa Anna reclaimed the Alamo Mission near San Antonio de Béxar.

questions.clue: French land forces under the Comte de Rochambeau and a French fleet under the Comte de Grasse combined with George Washington's Continental Army to win this 1781 Virginia engagement, the final major land battle of the Revolutionary War.

citations.title: Siege of Yorktown
citations.snippet: The Siege of Yorktown, also known as the Battle of Yorktown and the surrender at Yorktown, took place in 1781 and was the final major land engagement of the American Revolutionary War. It was won decisively by the Continental Army, led by G

questions.clue: Recognized as the leading force of the unified Greek military during the Greco-Persian Wars, this city-state's army made its legendary stand at Thermopylae in 480 BCE under King Leonidas.

citations.title: Sparta
citations.snippet: Sparta was a prominent city-state in Laconia in ancient Greece. In antiquity, the state was known as Lacedaemon, while Sparta referred to its capital, a group of villages in the valley of the Evrotas River in Laconia, in southeastern Pelopo

questions.clue: This type of 19th-century American West conflict typically arose from disputes over water rights, grazing rights, and the use of unfenced land that multiple competing ranchers tried to control.

citations.title: Range war

questions.clue: Settled as a Viking fishing village in the 10th century, this Scandinavian city became the capital of Denmark in the early 15th century and was bombarded by the British fleet during the Napoleonic Wars in 1807.

questions.clue: Finalized by the Continental Congress in November 1777 and ratified by all thirteen states by 1781, this document served as the new American nation's first governing framework before being superseded by the Constitution.
aliases: Articles of Confederation and Perpetual Union

citations.title: Articles of Confederation
citations.snippet: The Articles of Confederation, officially the Articles of Confederation and Perpetual Union, was an agreement and early body of law in the Thirteen Colonies, which served as the nation's first frame of government during the American Revolut

questions.clue: In this ancient Athenian procedure, citizens could vote — by scratching a name onto a broken piece of pottery — to banish a fellow citizen for ten years, often deployed preemptively against someone judged a threat to the state rather than to punish a specific crime.

citations.title: Ostracism

questions.clue: This American attorney, a leading member of the ACLU, defended Nathan Leopold and Richard Loeb in their 1924 murder trial and represented the biology teacher prosecuted in the 1925 Scopes 'monkey' trial.

citations.title: Clarence Darrow
citations.snippet: Clarence Seward Darrow was an American lawyer and politician who became famous in the 19th century for high-profile representations of trade union causes, and in the 20th century for several criminal matters, including the Leopold and Loeb

questions.clue: Triggered by the sinking of the USS Maine in Havana Harbor, this 1898 conflict ended with the United States acquiring Puerto Rico, Guam, and the Philippines from an empire that had held those territories for nearly four centuries.

citations.title: Spanish–American War
citations.snippet: The Spanish–American War was fought between Spain and the United States in 1898. It began with the sinking of the USS Maine in Havana Harbor in Cuba, and resulted in the U.S. acquiring sovereignty over Puerto Rico, Guam, and the Philippines

questions.clue: Arising from the Council of Trent (1545–1563), this movement produced new religious orders, stricter internal discipline, and doctrinal clarifications as the Roman Church's comprehensive answer to the Protestant upheaval sweeping northern Europe.

citations.title: Counter-Reformation

questions.clue: Following Germany's post-World War II division, this city served as the West German capital until reunification in 1990 returned the seat of government to Berlin.

citations.title: Berlin

questions.clue: This militia, formed in 1770 to defend the New Hampshire Grants from rival New York claims, was led by Ethan Allen and Benedict Arnold when it captured Fort Ticonderoga from the British in May 1775.

citations.title: Green Mountain Boys

questions.clue: This 18th-century Franciscan priest established eight of the 21 missions stretching from San Diego to San Francisco in Alta California and was beatified by Pope John Paul II in 1988.

citations.title: Junípero Serra

questions.clue: Construction of this freestanding Italian campanile began in the 12th century on ground too soft to support its weight, causing a tilt that worsened throughout its medieval completion — a structural fault not addressed until remedial work in the 1990s stabilized it to just under four degrees.

citations.title: Leaning Tower of Pisa
citations.snippet: The Leaning Tower of Pisa, or simply the Tower of Pisa, is the campanile, or freestanding bell tower, of Pisa Cathedral. It is known for its nearly four-degree lean, the result of an unstable foundation. The tower is one of three structures

questions.clue: Copenhagen served as the de facto capital and royal seat of this late medieval arrangement that bound Denmark, Sweden, and Norway under a shared monarch, governing most of the modern Nordic region.
